 {"id":"br-cwrh6","title":"Phase 4 VERIFY: Run Full TUI Inspector Suite — Polish Phase Gate","description":"# Phase 4 Verification Gate

## Purpose
After all Phase 4 tasks complete, verify no regressions.

## Mandatory Steps

### 1. Build verification
  go build ./cmd/ntm 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate4_build.log
  go vet ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate4_vet.log

### 2. Unit test regression check
  go test -v -count=1 -short ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate4_tests.log
  # Count passing tests
  PASS_COUNT=$(grep -c '^--- PASS' /tmp/gate4_tests.log)
  BASELINE_COUNT=$(grep -c '^--- PASS' /tmp/tui_test_baseline.log)
  echo "Tests: phase4=$PASS_COUNT baseline=$BASELINE_COUNT"
  # PASS_COUNT must be >= BASELINE_COUNT (may be higher due to new tests)
  if [ "$PASS_COUNT" -lt "$BASELINE_COUNT" ]; then
      echo "ERROR: Test count regression! $PASS_COUNT < $BASELINE_COUNT"
      exit 1
  fi

### 3. VHS capture suite
  bash /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/run_ntm_tui_suite.sh \
    --profiles ntm-dashboard-empty,ntm-dashboard-help,ntm-dashboard-navigate,ntm-dashboard-wide,ntm-palette \
    --suite-name "phase4_post_upgrade"

### 4. Snapshot comparison
  For each profile, compare phase4 vs previous phase snapshots.
  Log any visual differences.

### 5. Log summary
  Create /tmp/gate4_summary.json:
  {
    "phase": 4,
    "build": "pass|fail",
    "vet": "pass|fail",
    "test_count": N,
    "test_baseline": N,
    "test_regression": false,
    "vhs_profiles_pass": 5,
    "vhs_profiles_total": 5,
    "visual_regressions": []
  }

## Logging
- All commands log to /tmp/gate4_*.log
- Summary JSON at /tmp/gate4_summary.json
- VHS suite outputs at /tmp/tui_inspector/suites/phase4_post_upgrade/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] go build succeeds
- [ ] go vet clean (no warnings)
- [ ] Test count >= baseline (no regression)
- [ ] All 5 VHS profiles pass
- [ ] No visual regressions vs previous phase
- [ ] Summary JSON created with all metrics
- [ ] All logs archived

## Gate Policy
If ANY check fails, investigate and fix before proceeding to next phase.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:31:17.092146135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T00:24:37.406963279Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gate","phase-4","tui","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-cwrh6","depends_on_id":"br-27cl0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:26.139428459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-cwrh6","depends_on_id":"br-6g08x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:24.806002940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-cwrh6","depends_on_id":"br-eou3h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:27.902683047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-cwrh6","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:58.532383999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-dgfm7","title":"Phase 5a: Expand VHS Test Suite with Animation-Focused Tapes","description":"# Expand VHS Test Suite with Animation-Focused Tapes

## Background
Existing 5 VHS tapes cover basic rendering, navigation, resize, refresh, minimum size.
New tapes exercise the upgraded capabilities.

## IMPORTANT: VHS Does NOT Support Mouse Events
VHS only supports keyboard input (Type, Enter, Tab, Arrow keys, etc.). Mouse clicks and
scroll wheel CANNOT be tested via VHS. Mouse interaction must be verified via:
- Unit tests (TestMouseClickSelectsPane etc.)
- Manual testing in a real terminal

## New VHS Tapes to Create (6 tapes)

### 1. testdata/vhs/dashboard-toast-animation.tape
  Exercise toast spring animations. Wait for auto-dismiss timing.

### 2. testdata/vhs/dashboard-fuzzy-filter.tape
  Type "/" to enter filter mode, "cc" to filter, Esc to clear.

### 3. testdata/vhs/dashboard-table-scroll.tape
  Tab to history panel. Arrow down. PgDown. Verify table scrolling.

### 4. testdata/vhs/dashboard-focus-ring.tape
  Tab through all panels. Verify focus indicator (highlighted border) moves.
  Shift+Tab to reverse. Verify all visible panels are visited.

### 5. testdata/vhs/palette-fuzzy.tape
  Open palette. Type partial command. Verify filtering. Select.

### 6. testdata/vhs/dashboard-wide-layout.tape
  Set terminal to 2400 width (UltraWide tier). Verify layout adapts.

## TUI Inspector Profiles to Add

### /dp/tui_inspector/profiles/ntm-toast-animation.env
  profile_description="NTM toast spring animations"
  keys="sleep:4,?,sleep:2,Escape,sleep:2,q"

### /dp/tui_inspector/profiles/ntm-fuzzy-filter.env
  profile_description="NTM pane list fuzzy filtering"
  keys="sleep:3,/,sleep:500ms,c,c,sleep:2,Escape,sleep:1,q"

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED — tape validation)

### Test file: internal/tui/dashboard/vhs_tapes_test.go (new)
  func TestVHSTapesExist(t *testing.T) {
      expected := []string{
          "dashboard-basic.tape", "dashboard-navigation.tape",
          "dashboard-resize.tape", "dashboard-refresh.tape",
          "dashboard-minimum.tape",
          "dashboard-toast-animation.tape", "dashboard-fuzzy-filter.tape",
          "dashboard-table-scroll.tape", "dashboard-focus-ring.tape",
          "palette-fuzzy.tape", "dashboard-wide-layout.tape",
      }
      for _, tape := range expected {
          path := filepath.Join("testdata", "vhs", tape)
          if _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {
              t.Errorf("VHS tape missing: %s", tape)
          }
      }
  }

  func TestVHSTapesSyntaxValid(t *testing.T) {
      tapes, _ := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join("testdata", "vhs", "*.tape"))
      for _, tape := range tapes {
          data, err := os.ReadFile(tape)
          if err != nil { t.Errorf("cannot read %s: %v", tape, err); continue }
          content := string(data)
          if !strings.Contains(content, "Output") {
              t.Errorf("%s: missing Output directive", filepath.Base(tape))
          }
          if !strings.Contains(content, "Set Width") {
              t.Errorf("%s: missing Set Width", filepath.Base(tape))
          }
      }
  }

## Logging
- Log each tape creation with key sequence and duration
- Log VHS execution: exit code, video size, duration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 6 new VHS tapes created (NO mouse tape — VHS doesn't support mouse)
- [ ] 2 new TUI Inspector profiles created
- [ ] All tapes execute without VHS errors
- [ ] All tapes produce valid video
- [ ] Tape validation tests pass
- [ ] All existing 5 tapes still work
- [ ] New profiles integrate with suite runner

- testdata/vhs/dashboard-table-scroll.tape

- /dp/tui_inspector/profiles/ntm-fuzzy-filter.env

 {"id":"br-dja0e","title":"Phase 5b: Performance Profiling — Verify No Frame Drops from Spring Animations","description":"# Performance Profiling — Verify No Frame Drops from Spring Animations

## Background
Phase 4b adds springs across multiple render paths. Need to verify no performance regression.

## Profiling Strategy

### 1. View() Benchmark
  func BenchmarkDashboardView(b *testing.B) {
      m := New("test", "/tmp/test")
      m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 200, Height: 50})
      // Set up realistic data state
      m = populateTestData(m)
      b.ResetTimer()
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { _ = m.View() }
  }

### 2. Spring Tick Benchmark (already in Phase 4b but verify here)
  func BenchmarkSpringManagerTick50(b *testing.B) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      for i := 0; i < 50; i++ { sm.Set(fmt.Sprintf("s%d", i), float64(i)*10) }
      b.ResetTimer()
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { sm.Tick() }
  }

### 3. Frame Timing Test
  func TestViewRenderTime(t *testing.T) {
      m := New("test", "/tmp/test")
      m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 200, Height: 50})
      m = populateTestData(m)
      start := time.Now()
      for i := 0; i < 100; i++ { _ = m.View() }
      elapsed := time.Since(start)
      avgMs := float64(elapsed.Milliseconds()) / 100.0
      t.Logf("average View() time: %.2fms", avgMs)
      if avgMs > 16.0 {
          t.Errorf("View() too slow for 60fps: %.2fms (target <16ms)", avgMs)
      }
  }

### 4. Allocation Profile
  func BenchmarkDashboardViewAllocs(b *testing.B) {
      m := New("test", "/tmp/test")
      m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 200, Height: 50})
      m = populateTestData(m)
      b.ResetTimer()
      b.ReportAllocs()
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { _ = m.View() }
  }

## Performance Targets
- View() render time: < 16ms (60 FPS capable)
- View() allocations: < 200 allocs per frame
- SpringManager.Tick() with 50 springs: < 1μs
- CPU idle: < 0.5% of one core
- Memory overhead: negligible (< 10KB for spring state)

## If Performance Issues Found
1. Reduce spring count: batch springs (one per toast group, not per toast)
2. Lower FPS: FPS(30) — still smooth, half updates
3. Cache sub-renders: panels that haven't changed skip re-render
4. Use strings.Builder everywhere in View()

## Logging
- Log benchmark results to /tmp/ntm_perf_results.log
- Log frame timing violations: "SLOW FRAME: %dms (target 16ms)"
- Include in Phase 5c final report

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BenchmarkDashboardView exists and passes
- [ ] BenchmarkSpringManagerTick50 < 1μs
- [ ] TestViewRenderTime < 16ms average
- [ ] BenchmarkDashboardViewAllocs < 200 allocs/op
- [ ] No "SLOW FRAME" warnings during normal operation
- [ ] Performance targets documented in test output
- [ ] Results logged to /tmp/ntm_perf_results.log

## Files to Create
None — add benchmarks and perf tests to existing bench_test.go (149 LOC)
(AGENTS.md: no file proliferation; bench_test.go already exists for this purpose)

-{"id":"br-ee3h8","title":"Phase 2c: Add bubbles/viewport for Scrollable Panel Content","description":"# Add bubbles/viewport for Scrollable Panel Content

## Background — CORRECTED Understanding of scroll.go
internal/tui/components/scroll.go (141 LOC) is NOT a viewport wrapper. It provides:
  - ScrollState struct: FirstVisible, LastVisible, TotalItems
  - HasMoreAbove(), HasMoreBelow(), AllVisible() — query methods
  - Indicator() — returns "▲▼", "▲", "▼", or ""
  - RenderScrollIndicator(opts) — styled indicator
  - ScrollFooter(state, width) — footer bar with scroll info

Used by: panels/cass.go:229, panels/files.go:331, panels/history.go:821
These panels use scroll.go for RENDERING scroll indicators, not for managing scroll state.

## Plan: KEEP scroll.go, ADD new ScrollablePanel component

### scroll.go stays unchanged — indicator functions remain useful
### NEW: scrollable_panel.go wraps bubbles/viewport

## Implementation Plan

### 1. New file: internal/tui/components/scrollable_panel.go
  type ScrollablePanel struct {
      vp       viewport.Model
      content  string
      ready    bool
  }
  func NewScrollablePanel(w, h int) *ScrollablePanel {
      vp := viewport.New(w, h)
      vp.MouseWheelEnabled = true
      vp.MouseWheelDelta = 3
      return &ScrollablePanel{vp: vp, ready: true}
  }
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) SetContent(s string) { sp.vp.SetContent(s) }
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) SetSize(w, h int) { sp.vp.Width = w; sp.vp.Height = h }
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (*ScrollablePanel, tea.Cmd) { ... }
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) View() string { return sp.vp.View() }
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) ScrollPercent() float64 { return sp.vp.ScrollPercent() }
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) AtTop() bool { return sp.vp.AtTop() }
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) AtBottom() bool { return sp.vp.AtBottom() }
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) GotoTop() { sp.vp.GotoTop() }
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) GotoBottom() { sp.vp.GotoBottom() }

### 2. Integrate existing scroll.go indicators with viewport
  func (sp *ScrollablePanel) ScrollIndicator() string {
      state := ScrollState{
          FirstVisible: int(sp.vp.ScrollPercent() * float64(sp.totalLines())),
          TotalItems: sp.totalLines(),
      }
      return state.Indicator()
  }

### 3. Add HandlesOwnHeight to Panel interface
  type Panel interface {
      tea.Model
      SetSize(width, height int)
      Focus() / Blur()
      Config() PanelConfig
      Keybindings() []Keybinding
      HandlesOwnHeight() bool  // NEW
  }
  PanelBase default: return false (backward compatible)

### 4. Apply to panels — embed ScrollablePanel
  Alerts panel: when alert count > panel height, use viewport
  Beads panel: when bead count > panel height, use viewport
  History panel: if not using bubbles/table (Phase 2b), add viewport

### 5. truncateToHeight bypass
  In dashboard View(), check panel.HandlesOwnHeight() before truncating.

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### New file: internal/tui/components/scrollable_panel_test.go
  func TestScrollablePanelSetContent(t *testing.T) {
      sp := NewScrollablePanel(40, 10)
      sp.SetContent("line1\nline2\nline3")
      view := sp.View()
      if !strings.Contains(view, "line1") { t.Error("content not visible") }
  }

  func TestScrollablePanelOverflow(t *testing.T) {
      sp := NewScrollablePanel(40, 3)
      sp.SetContent(strings.Repeat("line\n", 20))
      if sp.AtBottom() { t.Error("should not be at bottom with overflow") }
  }

  func TestScrollablePanelPgDown(t *testing.T) {
      sp := NewScrollablePanel(40, 5)
      sp.SetContent(strings.Repeat("line\n", 50))
      sp, _ = sp.Update(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyPgDown})
      if sp.ScrollPercent() == 0 { t.Error("PgDown should scroll") }
  }

  func TestScrollablePanelResize(t *testing.T) {
      sp := NewScrollablePanel(40, 10)
      sp.SetContent(strings.Repeat("line\n", 20))
      sp.SetSize(80, 20)
      view := sp.View()
      if view == "" { t.Error("should render after resize") }
  }

  func TestScrollablePanelBoundaries(t *testing.T) {
      sp := NewScrollablePanel(40, 5)
      sp.SetContent(strings.Repeat("line\n", 50))
      if !sp.AtTop() { t.Error("should start at top") }
      sp.GotoBottom()
      if !sp.AtBottom() { t.Error("should be at bottom after GotoBottom") }
  }

  func TestScrollablePanelFitsNoScroll(t *testing.T) {
      sp := NewScrollablePanel(40, 20)
      sp.SetContent("short\ncontent")
      if sp.ScrollPercent() != 0 { t.Error("no scroll needed for short content") }
  }

  func TestScrollIndicatorIntegration(t *testing.T) {
      sp := NewScrollablePanel(40, 5)
      sp.SetContent(strings.Repeat("line\n", 50))
      ind := sp.ScrollIndicator()
      if ind == "" { t.Error("should show scroll indicator for overflow") }
  }

### Existing scroll_test.go (155 LOC) tests preserved — helper functions unchanged

### Panel interface compliance:
  func TestAlertsPanelHandlesOwnHeight(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewAlertsPanel()
      if !p.HandlesOwnHeight() { t.Error("should handle own height") }
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Run ntm-dashboard-navigate profile. Tab to alerts panel. PgDown/PgUp in video.

## Logging
- Log viewport creation: "scrollable panel: w=%d h=%d mouse=true"
- Debug log scroll events: "scroll: percent=%.1f%% top=%v bottom=%v"
- Log content overflow detection: "scrollable: %d lines, viewport %d lines"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] scroll.go UNCHANGED (indicator helpers preserved)
- [ ] New scrollable_panel.go wrapping bubbles/viewport
- [ ] Panel interface extended with HandlesOwnHeight() bool
- [ ] PanelBase returns false (backward compatible default)
- [ ] Mouse wheel scrolling works
- [ ] PgUp/PgDown/Home/End works
- [ ] Scroll indicator shows via existing scroll.go helpers
- [ ] truncateToHeight bypassed for viewport panels
- [ ] All existing scroll_test.go tests (155 LOC) pass
- [ ] All 7 new tests pass
- [ ] VHS shows panel scrolling

## Files to Create
- internal/tui/components/scrollable_panel.go (NEW)
- internal/tui/components/scrollable_panel_test.go (NEW)

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/panel.go (add HandlesOwnHeight to interface)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/alerts.go (embed ScrollablePanel)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/beads.go (embed ScrollablePanel)
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go (truncateToHeight bypass)

## Files UNCHANGED
- internal/tui/components/scroll.go (kept as-is)

## Risk: LOW-MEDIUM — New component is additive. HandlesOwnHeight() is a safe interface extension.

## SEQUENCING NOTE
This bead runs AFTER Phase 2a (help) because both modify dashboard.go. Phase 2a
changes the footer/help rendering; this bead changes the truncateToHeight bypass
in View(). Running after 2a prevents merge conflicts. Phase 2b (table) can run

 {"id":"br-eou3h","title":"Phase 4c: Enhanced Toast Notification System with Stacking and Dismiss","description":"# Enhanced Toast Notification System with Stacking and Dismiss

## Background
Building on Phase 1b (spring animations) and Phase 1a (mouse support). Upgrades from
simple appear/disappear to polished notification center.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Stacking with Spring Repositioning
  Each toast tracks Y offset with spring. On dismiss: update Y targets for remaining toasts.
  Springs animate them smoothly into new positions.

### 2. Click-to-Dismiss (requires Phase 1a mouse)
  Handle tea.MouseMsg in toast area. Click within bounding box → dismiss toast.
  Visual feedback: brief highlight on hover.

### 3. Progress Toasts
  New type: ProgressToast with updating progress bar:
  tm.PushProgress("spawn", "Spawning agents...", 0.0)
  tm.UpdateProgress("spawn", 0.5)
  tm.UpdateProgress("spawn", 1.0) // auto-dismiss after 1s

### 4. Persistent Toasts (don't auto-dismiss)
  tm.PushPersistent("auth_error", "Agent Mail auth failed", SeverityError)
  Only dismissed by user click or explicit tm.Dismiss("auth_error").

### 5. Toast History
  Ring buffer of last 20 dismissed toasts. Key 'n' shows history popup.

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Test file: internal/tui/components/toast_enhanced_test.go (new)
  func TestToastStackingReposition(t *testing.T) {
      tm := NewToastManager(4)
      tm.Push("a", "First", SeverityInfo)
      tm.Push("b", "Second", SeverityInfo)
      tm.Push("c", "Third", SeverityInfo)
      // Settle all springs
      for i := 0; i < 200; i++ { tm.Tick() }
      // Dismiss middle toast
      tm.Dismiss("b")
      // After ticking, remaining toasts should reposition
      for i := 0; i < 200; i++ { tm.Tick() }
      // Verify only 2 toasts remain
      if tm.Count() != 2 { t.Errorf("expected 2 toasts, got %d", tm.Count()) }
  }

  func TestToastClickDismiss(t *testing.T) {
      tm := NewToastManager(4)
      tm.Push("test", "Hello", SeverityInfo)
      for i := 0; i < 200; i++ { tm.Tick() } // settle
      // Simulate click at toast position
      dismissed := tm.HandleClick(5, 2) // x=5, y=2
      // Whether this dismisses depends on toast bounds
  }

  func TestProgressToast(t *testing.T) {
      tm := NewToastManager(4)
      tm.PushProgress("spawn", "Spawning...", 0.0)
      if tm.Count() != 1 { t.Error("expected 1 toast") }
      tm.UpdateProgress("spawn", 0.5)
      toast := tm.Find("spawn")
      if toast == nil { t.Fatal("toast not found") }
      if toast.Progress != 0.5 { t.Errorf("expected progress 0.5, got %f", toast.Progress) }
      tm.UpdateProgress("spawn", 1.0)
      // After completion + tick, should auto-dismiss
      for i := 0; i < 200; i++ { tm.Tick() }
  }

  func TestPersistentToast(t *testing.T) {
      tm := NewToastManager(4)
      tm.PushPersistent("error", "Auth failed", SeverityError)
      // Fast-forward past normal TTL
      for i := 0; i < 1000; i++ { tm.Tick() }
      if tm.Count() != 1 { t.Error("persistent toast should not auto-dismiss") }
      // Manual dismiss
      tm.Dismiss("error")
      for i := 0; i < 200; i++ { tm.Tick() }
      if tm.Count() != 0 { t.Error("dismissed persistent toast should be removed") }
  }

  func TestToastHistory(t *testing.T) {
      tm := NewToastManager(4)
      tm.Push("a", "First", SeverityInfo)
      tm.Dismiss("a")
      for i := 0; i < 200; i++ { tm.Tick() }
      history := tm.History()
      if len(history) != 1 { t.Errorf("expected 1 history entry, got %d", len(history)) }
      if history[0].ID != "a" { t.Error("wrong history entry") }
  }

  func TestToastHistoryMaxSize(t *testing.T) {
      tm := NewToastManager(4)
      for i := 0; i < 30; i++ {
          tm.Push(fmt.Sprintf("t%d", i), "msg", SeverityInfo)
          tm.Dismiss(fmt.Sprintf("t%d", i))
          for j := 0; j < 200; j++ { tm.Tick() }
      }
      if len(tm.History()) > 20 { t.Error("history should cap at 20") }
  }

 {"id":"br-fsoxa","title":"Attention Feed Phase 1d1: Add Agent Mail unread and ack-required attention signals","description":"# Why this bead exists

4. Keep the signals subordinate to Agent Mail as the source of truth; ntm is surfacing attention, not replacing the inbox.
5. Define honest absence semantics when Agent Mail visibility is unavailable or partial.

- Mail attention is high-value and should not be blocked on unrelated conflict-detection work.

- Detailed logs in tests that record the mail source state and why a signal was or was not emitted.

## Background
charmbracelet/harmonica v0.2.0 is in go.mod with ZERO imports. Toast system

## ACTUAL Toast API (verified from source)

      toasts []Toast

  }

  }

## Implementation Plan — EXTEND existing Tick(), don't replace

### 1. Add harmonica import
  import "github.com/charmbracelet/harmonica"

### 2. Add spring fields to Toast struct
  type Toast struct {
      // existing fields preserved
      ID, Message string
      Severity    ToastSeverity
      CreatedAt   time.Time
      TTL         time.Duration
      // NEW spring fields
      offsetX    float64
      offsetXVel float64
      spring     harmonica.Spring
      dismissed  bool
  }

### 3. Modify Push() to initialize spring
  func (tm *ToastManager) Push(toast Toast) {
      if styles.ReducedMotionEnabled() {
          toast.offsetX = 0.0
      } else {
          toast.spring = harmonica.NewSpring(harmonica.FPS(60), 6.0, 0.4)
          toast.offsetX = 40.0  // start offscreen right
      }
      // ... existing dedup logic preserved
  }

### 4. EXTEND Tick() — add spring updates before existing expiry logic
  func (tm *ToastManager) Tick() {
      // NEW: Update spring positions
      if !styles.ReducedMotionEnabled() {
          for i := range tm.toasts {
              t := &tm.toasts[i]
              target := 0.0
              if t.dismissed { target = 60.0 }
              t.offsetX, t.offsetXVel = t.spring.Update(t.offsetX, t.offsetXVel, target)
          }
          // Remove dismissed toasts that slid fully offscreen
          tm.removeSettledDismissed()
      }
      // EXISTING: Prune expired toasts (preserved from line 79)
      now := time.Now()
      active := tm.toasts[:0]
      for _, t := range tm.toasts {
          if now.Sub(t.CreatedAt) < t.TTL { active = append(active, t) }
      }
      tm.toasts = active
      // EXISTING: Clean old dedup entries (preserved)
      ...
  }

### 5. Modify RenderToasts() — apply offsetX
  In rendering, if offsetX > 0, shift toast rightward (or truncate from right).

### 6. Add IsAnimating() method
  func (tm *ToastManager) IsAnimating() bool {
      for _, t := range tm.toasts {
          if math.Abs(t.offsetX) > 0.5 || math.Abs(t.offsetXVel) > 0.1 { return true }
          if t.dismissed { return true }
      }
      return false
  }

### 7. Add Dismiss(id string) method
  func (tm *ToastManager) Dismiss(id string) {
      for i := range tm.toasts { if tm.toasts[i].ID == id { tm.toasts[i].dismissed = true } }
  }

 {"id":"br-ht8jy","title":"Overlay-Feed T4: Support --attention-cursor flag to open overlay pre-focused on specific event","description":"# Why this bead exists
A tending agent sometimes needs to bring a human directly to the attention item that triggered a wake-up. If the overlay can only open in a generic unfocused state, the human still has to hunt around and the handoff loses most of its value. This bead makes cursor-targeted overlay opening a first-class bridge between agent awareness and human awareness.

# Scope
1. Add an `--attention-cursor` path for opening the dashboard/overlay pre-focused on a specific attention item or the nearest surviving item.
2. Ensure the overlay focuses the attention panel and highlights the targeted item when it is still available.
3. Degrade honestly when the cursor is expired or no longer available by opening to the best current frontier rather than pretending exact continuity exists.
4. Provide a mechanical command shape that later robot-mode surfaces can emit directly as a suggested human-handoff action.
5. Keep cursor semantics aligned with the same namespace used by the attention feed and `--robot-attention` responses.

# Design considerations
- This feature is only useful if the cursor namespace is truly shared; any fork here would be a serious design bug.
- Focus/highlight behavior should make the handoff obvious without becoming visually obnoxious.
- Degraded behavior must be explicit so humans and agents do not infer false precision.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Unit tests for argument handling, focus selection, and cursor-found versus cursor-expired behavior.
- TUI snapshot/golden tests for focused/highlighted attention-panel rendering.
- Integration tests that prove robot attention responses can point to this surface mechanically.
- Transcript scenarios showing an agent wake, a suggested overlay command, and a human landing on the intended item or best-effort fallback.
- Detailed logs in tests that print requested cursor, located cursor, selected focus target, and degraded fallback choice.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cursor-targeted overlay opening exists and uses the same cursor namespace as the feed.
- [ ] The overlay focuses/highlights the intended attention item when possible.
- [ ] Expired or missing cursors degrade honestly and intelligibly.
- [ ] Automated tests and transcript artifacts make the handoff behavior easy to verify.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:07:58.617678724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T01:22:19.551511631Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","dashboard","overlay","phase-2","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ht8jy","depends_on_id":"br-5u235","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:10:28.434468366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-ht8jy","depends_on_id":"br-abp0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:10:29.400596235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-ht8jy","depends_on_id":"br-x66gc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:10:38.433829433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-x66gc","title":"Epic: Overlay–Attention Feed Integration — Bridge human and agent control surfaces","description":"# Strategic context
ntm is gaining two complementary surfaces for tending sessions:
1. an agent-facing attention feed (`--robot-events`, `--robot-digest`, `--robot-wait`, `--robot-attention`), and
2. human-facing live summaries in the dashboard/overlay family.

If those surfaces evolve independently, the product will feel schizophrenic: the agent will react to one frontier while the human sees another. This epic exists to make the overlay family consume the same attention model rather than inventing its own.

# Architectural position
This epic is subordinate to the core attention-feed work. It must not:
- create a second event taxonomy,
- replace the feed contract,
- introduce a parallel coordination system,
- or make overlay-specific state load-bearing for robot-mode correctness.

It should:
- reuse the same attention-summary/view-model where possible,
- make human attention state visible in the overlay family,
- add thin handoff signals that help human and agent operators avoid stepping on each other,
- and leave behind transcript/TUI artifacts that make regressions diagnosable.

# Child work covered here
- T1: attention items panel in the overlay
- T2: attention-aware badge, stats bar, and navigation hints
- T3: cursor-aware zoom/dismiss continuity events and hints
- T4: open overlay pre-focused on a specific attention cursor
- T5: integrate assign/watch workflow with overlay discoverability
- T6: add a robot-mode overlay handoff surface
- T7: verify the whole overlay/feed integration and record invariants

# Success criteria
- The overlay family surfaces the same meaningful attention frontier the agent-facing system does.
- Human actions in the overlay can be surfaced back into the attention feed honestly and mechanically.
- No overlay integration bead is allowed to fork the core taxonomy or silently duplicate signal logic.
- Each child bead leaves behind good golden/TUI/transcript artifacts so future regressions are obvious.

# Roll-up behavior
This bead is a roll-up epic, not an independently actionable task.  {"id":"br-xmmaj","title":"Attention Feed Phase 1a: Build the normalized event envelope, cursor allocator, and replay journal","description":"# Why this bead exists
`internal/events/bus.go` and `internal/tracker/tracker.go` already contain pieces of an event system, but neither one currently gives robot-mode clients a single replayable, cursor-addressable, monotonic feed. The foundation of this initiative is a normalized envelope plus a bounded journal that can answer "what happened since cursor N?" reliably.

# Scope
1. Introduce a normalized robot-event envelope type that can represent current bus events, tracker deltas, and future derived signals without schema churn.
2. Add a monotonic cursor allocator for attention-feed events.
3. Add a bounded in-memory journal / replay buffer that retains recent envelopes for cursor-based replay.
4. Define retention behavior and expiration explicitly. In v1, process restart or journal expiry should require resync via snapshot, not a new persistent queue.
5. Expose a single internal service that can append, replay, and subscribe, rather than having each robot surface build its own one-off logic.
6. Preserve existing event flow where practical; this bead should compose with the current bus/tracker instead of replacing them wholesale.
7. Add explicit metrics or debug-visible counters for replay-window size, dropped/evicted events, and oldest available cursor so later operators can debug cursor-expiry behavior.

# Design considerations
- Ordering must be deterministic enough for agent consumers to reason about cursors.
- The journal must be bounded so this feature cannot quietly become an unbounded memory sink.
- Concurrency behavior matters: concurrent publishers must still yield strictly monotonic cursor assignment.
- Restart behavior must be honest. If replay is no longer possible, return a structured resync instruction instead of pretending continuity exists.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Unit tests for monotonic cursor assignment under concurrent publish.
- Unit tests for replay windows, retention eviction, and cursor-expiration handling.
- Golden tests for success/error envelope serialization.
- Benchmarks or stress tests that exercise high event churn so we can confirm the journal remains bounded and cursor assignment remains ordered.
- Diagnostic logging in tests that prints cursor ranges, replay windows, and eviction decisions when assertions fail.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A normalized event envelope exists and is used by the new feed internals.
- [ ] Cursor assignment is monotonic and concurrency-safe.
- [ ] Replay since cursor works while the requested cursor remains in retention.
- [ ] Expired cursors produce a structured resync path instead of ambiguous empty output.
- [ ] The implementation remains bounded in memory and does not add a second durable store in v1.

 {"id":"br-xz14y","title":"Phase 1d: Replace Custom Progress Bar with bubbles/progress","description":"# Replace Custom Progress Bar with bubbles/progress

## Background — TWO SEPARATE PROGRESS BAR SYSTEMS EXIST

### System 1: styles package (styles/styles.go)
  - styles.ProgressBar(percent, width, filled, empty, colors...) string — line 340
  - styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent, width, filled, empty, tick, colors...) string — line 366
  Used by: dashboard.go:4313, layout.go:241, panels/quota.go:199, panels/accounts.go:295,301,
           panels/context.go:361

### System 2: components package (components/progress.go)
  - components.ProgressBar struct with Width, Percent, ShowPercent, ShowLabel, GradientColors, etc.
  - components.NewProgressBar(width) *ProgressBar
  - components.IndeterminateBar struct for unknown-duration progress
  Used by: synthesizer/modes_view.go:344, synthesis_progress.go:75, panels/ensemble.go:403

### NO GradientProgressBar function exists (previous bead was wrong)

## Implementation Plan — Hybrid (preserve both systems)

### For System 1 (styles.ProgressBar):
These are stateless render functions. Replace the simple 2-color case with bubbles/progress:
  func ProgressBar(percent float64, width int, filled, empty string, colors ...string) string {
      if len(colors) <= 2 {
          // Use bubbles/progress
          var p progress.Model
          if len(colors) == 2 {
              p = progress.New(progress.WithGradient(colors[0], colors[1]))
          } else {
              p = progress.New(progress.WithDefaultGradient())
          }
          p.Width = width
          p.ShowPercentage = false
          return p.ViewAs(percent)
      }
      // Keep custom implementation for 3+ color gradients
      return customGradientBar(percent, width, filled, empty, colors)
  }

ShimmerProgressBar stays as-is (bubbles doesn't support shimmer animation).

### For System 2 (components.ProgressBar struct):
Keep the struct API unchanged. Internally, when Animated is false and GradientColors has <=2
entries, delegate to bubbles/progress for rendering. Otherwise use custom.

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Expand: internal/tui/components/progress_test.go (already 304 LOC)
  func TestStylesProgressBar2Color(t *testing.T) {
      bar := styles.ProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", "#ff0000", "#0000ff")
      if bar == "" { t.Error("2-color bar empty") }
      if lipgloss.Width(bar) > 42 { t.Error("bar too wide") }
  }

  func TestStylesProgressBar3Color(t *testing.T) {
      bar := styles.ProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", "#ff0000", "#00ff00", "#0000ff")
      if bar == "" { t.Error("3-color bar empty") }
  }

  func TestStylesProgressBarZeroAndFull(t *testing.T) {
      bar0 := styles.ProgressBar(0.0, 40, "", "")
      bar1 := styles.ProgressBar(1.0, 40, "", "")
      if bar0 == "" || bar1 == "" { t.Error("boundary bars should render") }
  }

  func TestStylesShimmerProgressBar(t *testing.T) {
      bar := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", 5, nil)
      if bar == "" { t.Error("shimmer bar empty") }
  }

  func TestComponentsProgressBarStruct(t *testing.T) {
      pb := components.NewProgressBar(40)
      pb.Percent = 0.75
      view := pb.Render()
      if view == "" { t.Error("component bar empty") }
  }

  func TestComponentsIndeterminateBar(t *testing.T) {
      ib := components.NewIndeterminateBar(40)
      view := ib.Render()
      if view == "" { t.Error("indeterminate bar empty") }
  }

  func TestProgressBarReducedMotion(t *testing.T) {
      t.Setenv("NTM_ANIMATIONS", "0")
      bar1 := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", 0, nil)
      bar2 := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", 100, nil)
      // Reduced motion: shimmer tick should not affect output
  }

  func BenchmarkStylesProgressBar(b *testing.B) {
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
          _ = styles.ProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", "#ff0000", "#0000ff")
      }
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Progress bars visible in context panel and spawn panel. Capture ntm-dashboard-empty.

## Logging
- Debug log which backend used: "progress: using bubbles (2-color)" vs "progress: using custom (N-color)"
- Debug log when shimmer suppressed by ReducedMotion

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] styles.ProgressBar with <=2 colors uses bubbles/progress
- [ ] styles.ProgressBar with 3+ colors uses custom (preserved)
- [ ] styles.ShimmerProgressBar preserved (no change)
- [ ] components.ProgressBar struct API unchanged
- [ ] components.IndeterminateBar preserved
- [ ] All 10 call sites compile without changes
- [ ] All existing progress_test.go tests (304 LOC) pass
- [ ] New tests pass
- [ ] VHS shows correct rendering

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/styles/styles.go (ProgressBar function — lines 340-365)
- internal/tui/components/progress.go (optional: struct internal change)

## Risk: LOW — Two-system approach preserved.  {"id":"br-zqcqd","title":"Phase 3b: Refactor Panel Focus Management with Proper Focus Ring","description":"# Refactor Panel Focus Management with Proper Focus Ring

## Background
Dashboard uses integer focusedPanel with manual Tab cycling. Problems: hardcoded targets,
no skip-if-hidden, no visual focus indicator, Tab order doesn't follow layout.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. FocusRing type in internal/tui/dashboard/focus.go
  type FocusTarget struct {
      ID      string
      Panel   panels.Panel
      Visible func() bool
  }
  type FocusRing struct {
      targets  []FocusTarget
      current  int
      prevID   string
  }
  func (fr *FocusRing) Next() / Prev() — skip hidden targets
  func (fr *FocusRing) Current() FocusTarget
  func (fr *FocusRing) SetByID(id string) bool
  func (fr *FocusRing) Rebuild(targets []FocusTarget) — preserve focus if possible

### 2. Rebuild dynamically on tier change
### 3. Visual focus indicator (border color = theme.Primary when focused)
### 4. Spatial navigation (arrows = directional, stretch goal)
### 5. Focus follows mouse click (with Phase 1a)

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Test file: internal/tui/dashboard/focus_test.go (new)
  func TestFocusRingNext(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "c", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      if fr.Current().ID != "a" { t.Error("start at first") }
      fr.Next()
      if fr.Current().ID != "b" { t.Error("next to b") }
      fr.Next(); fr.Next()
      if fr.Current().ID != "a" { t.Error("wrap to a") }
  }

  func TestFocusRingSkipsHidden(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return false }},
          {ID: "c", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      fr.Next()
      if fr.Current().ID != "c" { t.Errorf("skip hidden b, got %s", fr.Current().ID) }
  }

  func TestFocusRingPrevWraps(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      fr.Prev()
      if fr.Current().ID != "b" { t.Error("prev wraps to last") }
  }

  func TestFocusRingAllHiddenNoInfiniteLoop(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return false }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return false }},
      })
      // Must not infinite loop — should stay at current index
      fr.Next()
      // Just verify it returned (didn't hang)
  }

  func TestFocusRingSetByID(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      if !fr.SetByID("b") { t.Error("should succeed") }
      if fr.Current().ID != "b" { t.Error("should focus b") }
      if fr.SetByID("nonexistent") { t.Error("should fail") }
  }

  func TestFocusRingRebuildPreservesFocus(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      fr.SetByID("b")
      fr.Rebuild([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "c", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      if fr.Current().ID != "b" { t.Error("should preserve focus on b") }
  }

  func TestFocusRingRebuildCurrentRemoved(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      fr.SetByID("b")
      // Rebuild WITHOUT b — focus should fall back to first visible
      fr.Rebuild([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "c", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      if fr.Current().ID != "a" { t.Errorf("should fall back to first, got %s", fr.Current().ID) }
  }

  func TestFocusRingEmpty(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing(nil)
      // Should handle gracefully
      fr.Next()
      fr.Prev()
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Run ntm-dashboard-navigate profile with Tab keystrokes.
Verify focus indicator (highlighted border) moves between panels.

## Logging
- Log focus transitions: "focus: %s -> %s"
- Log ring rebuild: "focus ring: rebuilt with %d targets (%d visible)"
- Log when current focus removed during rebuild: "focus ring: %s removed, falling back to %s"
- Debug log spatial navigation: "focus: arrow %s from %s -> %s"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FocusRing type with Next/Prev/SetByID/Rebuild
- [ ] Hidden panels skipped
- [ ] Visual focus indicator on focused panel
- [ ] Focus preserved across Rebuild (by ID)
- [ ] Graceful fallback when focused panel removed in Rebuild
- [ ] No infinite loop when all targets hidden
- [ ] Empty ring handled gracefully
- [ ] All 8 tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] VHS shows focus indicator

## Files to Create
- internal/tui/dashboard/focus.go
- internal/tui/dashboard/focus_test.go

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/update.go (Tab handler uses FocusRing)
- internal/tui/dashboard/view_panels.go (focus indicator rendering)
